verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 encodes the type 1 inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate receptor, a large tetrameric calcium-release channel resident in the endoplasmic reticulum membrane. Its cytoplasmic face ligand-binding domain faces the cytosol; the channel pore spans the ER membrane; no portion is topologically exposed to the extracellular space. Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-surface-directed therapeutic program targets ITPR1 protein body extracellularly; (2) not an ectodomain-shedding TM precursor — it is an ER-resident channel with no extracellular domain; (3) no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to the PM outer leaflet documented; (4) no documented PM minority pool — it is strictly ER-localized; (5) no gene-specific evidence for cancer/oncogenic outer-leaflet display, cell-death-induced surface exposure, germline-restricted PM display, or activation-induced PM translocation. Gene-group (IP3R family) and NCBI summary both consistently point to ER-resident intracellular calcium channel. No CD designation. All five contextual buckets ruled out.
